title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a bounded concurrent report pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.
Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Pipeline Fanout Fanin
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Pipeline 3 starts 5 workers, has a two-slot buffer, one early consumer exit, two simultaneously ready select cases, and one unclosed output. Trace ownership, blocking, close authority, result order, and every goroutine's stop path. Competency probe: Build bounded fan-out and fan-in stages with one ownership model, ordered-result policy, error propagation, and leak checks.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Pipeline Fanout Fanin.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build bounded fan-out and fan-in stages with one ownership model, ordered-result policy, error propagation, and leak checks. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Pipeline Fanout Fanin, keep Pipeline Fanout Fanin connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
